denial_letter_text:
Cigna Medical Management — Neurology Utilization Review
Member W136470198 | UM-2026-136470 | DOS 22 May 2026

Determination: Not medically necessary (CO-96)

Screening summary: The utilization team reviewed a surgical authorization request. Per standard workflow, the requested service. No approval is issued for optic nerve sheath fenestration at this time.

Policy basis — step therapy sequence incomplete
Cigna coverage requires documented failure of prerequisite medical management before fenestration for idiopathic intracranial hypertension (G93.2). The file does not demonstrate completion of required acetazolamide optimization and structured weight-management counseling prior to surgical referral.

Clinical identifiers supplied on the request form list idiopathic intracranial hypertension and fenestration; detailed papilledema grading and visual field data were not referenced in the determination narrative.

Information request: Claim materials may be obtained through the member portal.

Appeal: Written appeal may be submitted within 180 days citing UM-2026-136470.

Issued 3 June 2026 — Cigna Neurology Medical Review
clinical_context: A 47-year-old woman with idiopathic intracranial hypertension (G93.2) completed eleven weeks of acetazolamide 2 g daily yet retains papilledema grade III with 8 dB mean deviation loss on Humphrey perimetry. Structured weight counseling produced only 1.2 kg change. Neuro-ophthalmology recommends optic nerve sheath fenestration after documented acetazolamide failure and a symptomatic kidney stone limiting further diuretic titration. Records were faxed 19 May 2026 before the plan's review deadline.

diversity_delta.reasoning: IIH fenestration step-therapy arc differs from neighbour migraine CGRP and MS DMT cases.

safety_redactor.reasoning: Commercial-plan denial for an in-scope insurer with no banned-topic language, no violence, and no out-of-scope programs.

flaw_injection_verifier_mid.reasoning: Opening uses the requested service without papilledema detail; no ERISA/1133/civil-action language in appeal block.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: The denial is clearly algorithmic boilerplate and omits ERISA disclosures. The clinical case strongly supports PT. It should be easily appealable.
exploitable_weaknesses: failure to engage with clinical records

synthetic_provenance.human_summary: Manual swarm case for Cigna Medical Necessity / neurology / sub_tactic=step_therapy_missing. Producers and critics executed by Cursor agent intelligence (no Vertex Gemini).
